title: physostegia virginiana var. formosior
intro: Physostegia virginiana var. formosior, also known as tall skullcap or Formosa beardtongue, is a perennial plant species belonging to the borage family, characterized by its tall spikes of purple flowers and unique shape, which resembles a skull. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its flavonoid and phenolic acid content. The health benefits of Physostegia virginiana var. formosior include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, from colds and flu to digestive problems.
